summary.text: Deterministic mock analysis of "Checkout failures after v2.4.1 deployment" based on 14 evidence item(s) affecting checkout-api.
summary.impact: Severity was classified as critical. Actual user/business impact should be confirmed against the evidence.

timeline.title: feature-flag-log
timeline.description: Feature flag 'new-tax-calculation' enabled for 5% of traffic as part of an ongoing experiment.

timeline.title: cache-warmer.log
timeline.description: WARN: Redis key eviction rate elevated for session cache.

timeline.title: deploy-log-checkout-api
timeline.description: Deploy v2.4.1 to checkout-api completed at 14:28 UTC. Included: updated payment retry logic, DB connection pool size reduced from 50 to 20 per pod (part of a cost-reduction pass), and minor dependency bumps.

timeline.title: payment-gateway-adapter.log
timeline.description: WARN: upstream payment provider (Stripe) responding with elevated latency, avg 2100ms.

timeline.title: checkout-api-error.log
timeline.description: POST /api/checkout/submit -- 47 failures between 14:33 and 14:55 UTC. Error: 'DB connection timeout after 5000ms'.

timeline.title: postgres-primary.log
timeline.description: FATAL: remaining connection slots are reserved -- logged 12 times between 14:34 and 14:50 UTC.

timeline.title: Support ticket queue
timeline.description: 5 tickets between 14:35 and 15:00 UTC: 'Card declined unexpectedly', 'Checkout page spinner never stops', 'Got a 500 error page', 'Tried 3 times, same error', 'Is the store down?'.

timeline.title: Datadog
timeline.description: checkout-api p95 latency exceeded 4000ms (threshold 800ms). Triggered 14:36 UTC, resolved 15:10 UTC.

timeline.title: Datadog
timeline.description: checkout-api error rate reached 18% (threshold 2%). Triggered 14:37 UTC.

timeline.title: Datadog
timeline.description: checkout-api pod count auto-scaled from 10 to 14 due to sustained CPU pressure.

timeline.title: Incident report
timeline.description: Customers have been unable to complete checkout since roughly 14:30 UTC. Multiple support tickets mention a "Payment could not be processed" error on the final checkout step. The checkout-api service was deployed shortly before reports started coming in.

timeline.title: checkout-api.log
timeline.description: INFO: checkout completed successfully for order #88213 (200 OK, 320ms).

facts.statement: payment-gateway-adapter.log reported: WARN: upstream payment provider (Stripe) responding with elevated latency, avg 2100ms.
facts.explanation: Directly observed in evidence item sample-ecommerce-checkout-ev-07 (application log).

facts.statement: Datadog reported: checkout-api pod count auto-scaled from 10 to 14 due to sustained CPU pressure.
facts.explanation: Directly observed in evidence item sample-ecommerce-checkout-ev-12 (monitoring alert).

facts.statement: checkout-api-error.log reported: POST /api/checkout/submit -- 47 failures between 14:33 and 14:55 UTC. Error: 'DB connection timeout after 5000ms'.
facts.explanation: Directly observed in evidence item sample-ecommerce-checkout-ev-03 (api error).

assumptions.statement: It is possible that this is related to the incident: Deploy v2.4.1 to checkout-api completed at 14:28 UTC. Included: updated payment retry logic, DB connection pool size reduced from 50 to 20 per pod (part of a cost-reduction pass), and minor dependency bumps.
assumptions.explanation: Based on deployment note evidence item sample-ecommerce-checkout-ev-02; this has not been confirmed.

assumptions.statement: It is possible that this is related to the incident: Feature flag 'new-tax-calculation' enabled for 5% of traffic as part of an ongoing experiment.

assumptions.statement: It is possible that this is related to the incident: 5 tickets between 14:35 and 15:00 UTC: 'Card declined unexpectedly', 'Checkout page spinner never stops', 'Got a 500 error page', 'Tried 3 times, same error', 'Is the store down?'.
assumptions.explanation: Based on user report evidence item sample-ecommerce-checkout-ev-08; this has not been confirmed.

assumptions.statement: It is possible that this is related to the incident: This same connection-timeout error signature has appeared briefly a few times over the past month, always self-resolving within minutes with no deploy involved. Exact prior dates were not logged anywhere searchable.

assumptions.statement: It is possible that this is related to the incident: Per-request database-connection-hold-time metrics are not currently instrumented for checkout-api, so it is not possible to directly confirm from existing dashboards whether individual requests are holding connections for longer than before the deploy.
assumptions.explanation: Based on other evidence item sample-ecommerce-checkout-ev-14; this has not been confirmed.
